config.systemPrompt:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

methodology: One read-only external-project analysis task executed through a DeepSeek LLM agent; it is operationally measured but not success-scored.

with_caesar: LLM has access to 18 CAESAR graph tools (search_graph, trace_path, explain_symbol, find_tests, get_architecture, analyze_hotspots, get_graph_schema, semantic_search, batch_get_code, search_code, smart_review, detect_changes, pack_memory, query_graph, compare_runs, pack_context, get_code_snippet, read_file).
without_caesar: LLM has access to read_file + grep only.
Fresh conversation per task x condition x run. No shared history or cache between conditions.
Paid microbenchmark protocol: exact deepseek-v4-flash, temperature 0, fixed per-round seeds, identical prompts/order/limits/runtime; no model or provider fallback.
Mode: live API calls. Suite: external-project.
Cost is estimated from token usage and configured pricing. Wall time includes API latency.
ROI claims blocked when baseline invalid or sample size too small.

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

Identify one representative user-facing workflow in WORKMAT and trace it end to end through the code. Do not assume which tools to use. Explain the initiating action, control flow, state transitions, external boundaries, and failure points, citing concrete files and symbols. Respond with JSON: {"workflow_name":"...","flow":["..."],"state_transitions":["..."],"external_boundaries":["..."],"failure_points":["..."],"evidence":["..."],"summary":"..."}.

tasks.response:
I now have a thorough understanding of the complete end-to-end flow. Here is the answer:

```json
{
  "workflow_name": "Chat Conversation (Customer → AI → Lead Capture)",
  "flow": [
    "User clicks chat trigger → ChatWidget opens (src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx, line 2104, open useEffect)",
    "Widget initializes: loads chat settings from /api/chat/settings, checks localStorage consent, generates conversationKey + visitorKey, clears lead/contact storage for non-Vapi providers (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 1852–1965)",
    "Greeting messages queued via queueAssistantMessages → typeAssistantMessage (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 2050–2100)",
    "User types message in <textarea> and submits via handleSubmit (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 2375–2590, async function)",
    "handleSubmit builds request payload (input, history, contactMemory, leadMemory, locale, conversationKey, visitorKey, user hint)",
    "POST request sent to /api/chat (src/app/api/chat/route.ts, POST handler at line 3135)",
    "POST reads ChatRuntimeSettings → routes to one of: Vapi proxy, requestLocalChatReply, or requestOpenAiAssistantReply",
    "requestLocalChatReply (line 3126): normalizes history, hydrates contact/lead memory from history, infers expected fields, builds compact system prompt via buildCompactChatSystemPrompt, iterates through local model candidates, calls requestAiChatAttempt with prompt, parses <json> extraction block and <tools> block, filters duplicate tool calls, persists conversation via persistLocalConversation → Prisma ChatSession + ChatMessage, handles farewell detection → closeLocalChatSession",
    "requestOpenAiAssistantReply (line 3365): similar normalization, calls openAiFetchJson to POST to /api.openai.com/v1/responses with Response API, parses function_calls output, iterates attempts (max 6), persists conversation, handles farewell → closeOpenAiChatSession",
    "Response returned to ChatWidget → extractReply parses reply, processToolCalls extracts tool calls (contact/lead data), persistContactMemory/LeadMemory writes to localStorage",
    "Farewell or inactivity triggers closeChat (line 2180) → notifySessionClosed sends PATCH /api/chat (line 3135 PATCH handler) → findChatSessionForClose → closeTrackedChatSession (line 1780)",
    "closeTrackedChatSession updates session status, calls syncChatSessionLeadState (src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts, line 545) → extracts lead fields from messages, creates/updates Prisma Lead + LeadEvent records",
    "Sends email notification via sendSmtpMail (if SMTP configured), appends to Google Sheets (if OpenAI provider and Sheets configured)"
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "closed → opening (user click sets open=true)",
    "opening → consent_pending (if localStorage consent missing → ChatConsentModal shows)",
    "consent_pending → ready (user accepts → consent stored in localStorage)",
    "ready → greeting (queueAssistantMessages emits greeting bubbles)",
    "greeting → awaiting_input (greeting displayed, textarea focused)",
    "awaiting_input → processing (user submits → loading=true, scheduleAssistantPresence shows 'Escribiendo...')",
    "processing → replying (AI reply received → typeAssistantMessage reveals assistant bubble)",
    "replying → awaiting_input (message displayed, loading=false, inactivity timers restart)",
    "any → farewell_countdown (assistant or user farewell language detected → 15s countdown shown)",
    "farewell_countdown → closed (countdown expires → closeChat('farewell') → session.status='completed')",
    "any → inactivity_warning (inactivityInitialWarningSeconds elapsed → warning message + periodic repeats)",
    "inactivity_warning → inactivity_closing (inactivityAutoCloseSeconds elapsed → final message + closeChat('inactivity') → session.status='closed')",
    "any → closed (user clicks X → closeChat('manual') → onClose callback)"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "OpenAI Responses API (api.openai.com/v1/responses) — called by requestOpenAiAssistantReply via openAiFetchJson",
    "Local LLM endpoint (configurable baseUrl/chatEndpoint from Admin AI settings) — called by requestAiChatAttempt",
    "Vapi API proxy (TARGET=/api/vapi_chat) — forwarded by POST handler when provider='vapi'",
    "Prisma/PostgreSQL database — ChatSession, ChatMessage, Visitor, Lead, LeadEvent tables via prisma client (src/lib/prisma.ts)",
    "SMTP email server — sendSmtpMail in closeTrackedChatSession sends lead notification to configured email (cesarmesaspain@gmail.com)",
    "Google Sheets API (sheets.googleapis.com) — appendClosedChatLeadToSheet appends lead row to spreadsheet (OpenAI provider only)",
    "localStorage (browser) — chat consent flag, conversationKey, lastResponseId, visitorKey, contactMemory, leadMemory",
    "EventSource SSE stream at /api/chat/actions — real-time tool call delivery from chatActionBus"
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Empty user input → 400 'Mensaje vacío' (requestLocalChatReply, requestOpenAiAssistantReply)",
    "Local LLM unreachable → iterates all model candidates, then returns 502 'LOCAL_CHAT_FAILED'",
    "Local LLM SSL wrong version → hasWrongVersionError triggers automatic retry with http:// fallback (fetchWithFallback)",
    "OpenAI API key missing → 500 'OpenAI no está configurado'",
    "OpenAI API call failure → retries up to 6 attempts, then 502 'OPENAI_RESPONSE_FAILED'",
    "OpenAI HTTP error → nested error extraction from response.error, throws specific error code",
    "Timeout → AbortController with configurable timeoutMs; timeout produces 'CHAT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT' error on client",
    "Prisma persistLocalConversation failure → caught and logged, non-fatal to chat reply",
    "SMTP not configured → closeTrackedChatSession records emailError='smtp_not_configured', skips email",
    "SMTP send failure → emailError set to error message, logged, delivery continues",
    "Google Sheets auth failure → sheetError set, logged; append fails silently for non-OpenAI providers",
    "SSE connection drop → eventSource.onerror triggers scheduleRetry with 1500ms delay, up to 3 retries",
    "Farewell race conditions → closeSyncedRef prevents double-close; closeReason deduplication in closing logic"
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx — useLanguage function (line 285–3277): entire UI, handleSubmit (line 2375), closeChat (line 2180), processToolCalls (line 1750), SSE EventSource setup (line 1990)",
    "src/app/api/chat/route.ts — POST handler (line 3135), requestLocalChatReply (line 3126–3363), requestOpenAiAssistantReply (line 3365–3588), closeTrackedChatSession (line 1780), persistLocalConversation (line 1440)",
    "src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts — syncChatSessionLeadState (line 545–968): extracts lead from chat messages, creates/updates Prisma Lead records",
    "src/lib/mailing/service.ts — sendSmtpMail (used in closeTrackedChatSession for email notification)",
    "src/app/api/chat/actions/route.ts — SSE endpoint streaming tool calls from chatActionBus",
    "src/lib/chatActionBus.ts — subscribeChatActions (line 73): event-based tool dispatch via EventEmitter",
    "src/lib/chatActionAuth.ts — hasChatActionTokenSecret, verifyChatActionToken for SSE auth",
    "src/app/api/chat/settings/route.ts — chat settings read/write endpoint",
    "src/lib/companyProfile.ts — readChatEnabledCompanyProfile for public contact info in system prompt",
    "src/lib/postalLookup.ts — postal code → locality resolution used in lead qualification"
  ],
  "summary": "The Chat Conversation workflow is the primary user-facing interaction in WORKMAT. A website visitor opens the ChatWidget (React component), types a message, which is POSTed to /api/chat. Based on admin-configured provider (Vapi, OpenAI, or local LLM), the backend routes to the appropriate AI service. The system maintains session state via Prisma ChatSession/ChatMessage models, incrementally builds contact and lead memory through NLP inference functions (inferWorkType, inferZone, inferUrgency, etc.), and applies a deriveConversationPolicy classifier to determine intent (service/greeting/farewell/business/informational) and goal (qualify/capture_contact/resolve/close). When a conversation ends (farewell or inactivity), the closeTrackedChatSession function extracts a structured lead, writes it to the Lead table, sends an HTML email notification via SMTP, and appends the lead to Google Sheets (OpenAI provider only). Real-time tool calls for lead data capture are streamed to the ChatWidget via SSE at /api/chat/actions. The workflow has 8 external boundaries (OpenAI API, local LLM, Vapi proxy, PostgreSQL, SMTP, Google Sheets, browser localStorage, SSE) and 14+ failure points with defensive retries and fallback logic."
}
```

flow: User clicks chat trigger → ChatWidget opens (src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx, line 2104, open useEffect)

Widget initializes: loads chat settings from /api/chat/settings, checks localStorage consent, generates conversationKey + visitorKey, clears lead/contact storage for non-Vapi providers (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 1852–1965)
Greeting messages queued via queueAssistantMessages → typeAssistantMessage (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 2050–2100)
User types message in <textarea> and submits via handleSubmit (ChatWidget.tsx, lines 2375–2590, async function)
handleSubmit builds request payload (input, history, contactMemory, leadMemory, locale, conversationKey, visitorKey, user hint)
POST request sent to /api/chat (src/app/api/chat/route.ts, POST handler at line 3135)
POST reads ChatRuntimeSettings → routes to one of: Vapi proxy, requestLocalChatReply, or requestOpenAiAssistantReply

requestLocalChatReply (line 3126): normalizes history, hydrates contact/lead memory from history, infers expected fields, builds compact system prompt via buildCompactChatSystemPrompt, iterates through local model candidates, calls requestAiChatAttempt with prompt, parses <json> extraction block and <tools> block, filters duplicate tool calls, persists conversation via persistLocalConversation → Prisma ChatSession + ChatMessage, handles farewell detection → closeLocalChatSession

requestOpenAiAssistantReply (line 3365): similar normalization, calls openAiFetchJson to POST to /api.openai.com/v1/responses with Response API, parses function_calls output, iterates attempts (max 6), persists conversation, handles farewell → closeOpenAiChatSession
Response returned to ChatWidget → extractReply parses reply, processToolCalls extracts tool calls (contact/lead data), persistContactMemory/LeadMemory writes to localStorage
Farewell or inactivity triggers closeChat (line 2180) → notifySessionClosed sends PATCH /api/chat (line 3135 PATCH handler) → findChatSessionForClose → closeTrackedChatSession (line 1780)

closeTrackedChatSession updates session status, calls syncChatSessionLeadState (src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts, line 545) → extracts lead fields from messages, creates/updates Prisma Lead + LeadEvent records
Sends email notification via sendSmtpMail (if SMTP configured), appends to Google Sheets (if OpenAI provider and Sheets configured)
state_transitions: closed → opening (user click sets open=true)
opening → consent_pending (if localStorage consent missing → ChatConsentModal shows)
consent_pending → ready (user accepts → consent stored in localStorage)
ready → greeting (queueAssistantMessages emits greeting bubbles)
greeting → awaiting_input (greeting displayed, textarea focused)
awaiting_input → processing (user submits → loading=true, scheduleAssistantPresence shows 'Escribiendo...')
processing → replying (AI reply received → typeAssistantMessage reveals assistant bubble)
replying → awaiting_input (message displayed, loading=false, inactivity timers restart)
any → farewell_countdown (assistant or user farewell language detected → 15s countdown shown)
farewell_countdown → closed (countdown expires → closeChat('farewell') → session.status='completed')
any → inactivity_warning (inactivityInitialWarningSeconds elapsed → warning message + periodic repeats)
inactivity_warning → inactivity_closing (inactivityAutoCloseSeconds elapsed → final message + closeChat('inactivity') → session.status='closed')
any → closed (user clicks X → closeChat('manual') → onClose callback)
external_boundaries: OpenAI Responses API (api.openai.com/v1/responses) — called by requestOpenAiAssistantReply via openAiFetchJson
Local LLM endpoint (configurable baseUrl/chatEndpoint from Admin AI settings) — called by requestAiChatAttempt
Vapi API proxy (TARGET=/api/vapi_chat) — forwarded by POST handler when provider='vapi'
Prisma/PostgreSQL database — ChatSession, ChatMessage, Visitor, Lead, LeadEvent tables via prisma client (src/lib/prisma.ts)
SMTP email server — sendSmtpMail in closeTrackedChatSession sends lead notification to configured email (cesarmesaspain@gmail.com)
Google Sheets API (sheets.googleapis.com) — appendClosedChatLeadToSheet appends lead row to spreadsheet (OpenAI provider only)
localStorage (browser) — chat consent flag, conversationKey, lastResponseId, visitorKey, contactMemory, leadMemory
EventSource SSE stream at /api/chat/actions — real-time tool call delivery from chatActionBus
failure_points: Empty user input → 400 'Mensaje vacío' (requestLocalChatReply, requestOpenAiAssistantReply)
Local LLM unreachable → iterates all model candidates, then returns 502 'LOCAL_CHAT_FAILED'
Local LLM SSL wrong version → hasWrongVersionError triggers automatic retry with http:// fallback (fetchWithFallback)
OpenAI API key missing → 500 'OpenAI no está configurado'
OpenAI API call failure → retries up to 6 attempts, then 502 'OPENAI_RESPONSE_FAILED'
OpenAI HTTP error → nested error extraction from response.error, throws specific error code
Timeout → AbortController with configurable timeoutMs; timeout produces 'CHAT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT' error on client
Prisma persistLocalConversation failure → caught and logged, non-fatal to chat reply
SMTP not configured → closeTrackedChatSession records emailError='smtp_not_configured', skips email
SMTP send failure → emailError set to error message, logged, delivery continues
Google Sheets auth failure → sheetError set, logged; append fails silently for non-OpenAI providers
SSE connection drop → eventSource.onerror triggers scheduleRetry with 1500ms delay, up to 3 retries
Farewell race conditions → closeSyncedRef prevents double-close; closeReason deduplication in closing logic
evidence: src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx — useLanguage function (line 285–3277): entire UI, handleSubmit (line 2375), closeChat (line 2180), processToolCalls (line 1750), SSE EventSource setup (line 1990)

src/app/api/chat/route.ts — POST handler (line 3135), requestLocalChatReply (line 3126–3363), requestOpenAiAssistantReply (line 3365–3588), closeTrackedChatSession (line 1780), persistLocalConversation (line 1440)
src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts — syncChatSessionLeadState (line 545–968): extracts lead from chat messages, creates/updates Prisma Lead records
src/lib/mailing/service.ts — sendSmtpMail (used in closeTrackedChatSession for email notification)
src/app/api/chat/actions/route.ts — SSE endpoint streaming tool calls from chatActionBus
src/lib/chatActionBus.ts — subscribeChatActions (line 73): event-based tool dispatch via EventEmitter
src/lib/chatActionAuth.ts — hasChatActionTokenSecret, verifyChatActionToken for SSE auth
src/app/api/chat/settings/route.ts — chat settings read/write endpoint
src/lib/companyProfile.ts — readChatEnabledCompanyProfile for public contact info in system prompt
src/lib/postalLookup.ts — postal code → locality resolution used in lead qualification
result.summary: The Chat Conversation workflow is the primary user-facing interaction in WORKMAT. A website visitor opens the ChatWidget (React component), types a message, which is POSTed to /api/chat. Based on admin-configured provider (Vapi, OpenAI, or local LLM), the backend routes to the appropriate AI service. The system maintains session state via Prisma ChatSession/ChatMessage models, incrementally builds contact and lead memory through NLP inference functions (inferWorkType, inferZone, inferUrgency, etc.), and applies a deriveConversationPolicy classifier to determine intent (service/greeting/farewell/business/informational) and goal (qualify/capture_contact/resolve/close). When a conversation ends (farewell or inactivity), the closeTrackedChatSession function extracts a structured lead, writes it to the Lead table, sends an HTML email notification via SMTP, and appends the lead to Google Sheets (OpenAI provider only). Real-time tool calls for lead data capture are streamed to the ChatWidget via SSE at /api/chat/actions. The workflow has 8 external boundaries (OpenAI API, local LLM, Vapi proxy, PostgreSQL, SMTP, Google Sheets, browser localStorage, SSE) and 14+ failure points with defensive retries and fallback logic.

args_redacted.query: chat widget open function send message

messages.content:
You are a code intelligence agent. You help developers understand code by answering questions precisely and concisely.

Rules:
- Answer based on the tools and information available.
- When MCP tools are available, their names, descriptions, and argument schemas are authoritative. Choose and use them freely when they provide relevant evidence; do not assume undocumented behavior.
- When using tools, call them one at a time. Wait for each result before proceeding.
- Be thorough: verify your answer before responding.
- When you have enough information, provide a concise final answer.
- Do NOT invent information. If you cannot find something, say so.
- Output your final answer as a single JSON object with the exact fields requested. Nothing else.

Identify one representative user-facing workflow in WORKMAT and trace it end to end through the code. Do not assume which tools to use. Explain the initiating action, control flow, state transitions, external boundaries, and failure points, citing concrete files and symbols. Respond with JSON: {"workflow_name":"...","flow":["..."],"state_transitions":["..."],"external_boundaries":["..."],"failure_points":["..."],"evidence":["..."],"summary":"..."}.

tasks.response:
Here is the trace of the most representative user-facing workflow in WORKMAT: the **AI Chat → Lead Capture → Notification** flow.

```json
{
  "workflow_name": "AI Chat → Lead Capture → Notification",
  "flow": [
    "1. User visits website → `src/app/page.tsx` renders `<HomeApp>` with hero layout. The hero CTA button ('Habla con WORKMAT') calls `handleOpenChat(null)` in `src/app/components/HomeApp.tsx` (line ~214).",
    "2. `handleOpenChat` sets `chatBooted=true`, `chatOpen=true` → this mounts `<ChatWidget open={true} ... >` in `HomeApp.tsx` (line ~439).",
    "3. ChatWidget (`src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx`) initializes: checks chat consent (`ChatConsentModal`), generates a `conversationKey` via `crypto.randomUUID()`, stores it in localStorage as `workmat-conversation-key`, and sends opening greeting (`queueAssistantMessages` with greeting lines).",
    "4. User types a message and presses Enter → `handleSubmit()` in ChatWidget (line ~1046) validates input, calls `scheduleAssistantPresence(text)` to show 'Escribiendo...', then `POST`s JSON to `/api/chat` via `fetchWithTimeout`.",
    "5. The API route `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` → `POST()` (line ~2680) reads runtime settings via `readChatRuntimeSettings()`. If provider is `'local'` → `requestLocalChatReply()` (line ~2294); if `'openai'` → `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` (line ~2499); if `'vapi'` → proxies to the Vapi endpoint.",
    "6. In `requestLocalChatReply()`: the system prompt is built by `buildCompactChatSystemPrompt()` (line ~2185). The message history + new input is sent to the local LLM (Qwen etc.) via `requestAiChatAttempt()`. The LLM returns an answer containing `<json>...</json>` extraction and possibly `<tools>...</tools>` tool calls.",
    "7. The answer is parsed by `parseLocalModelExtraction()` (extract structured lead/contact data) and `parseLocalToolCalls()` (extract function calls like `enviar_nombre_usuario`, `guardar_tipo_trabajo`, etc.). Tool calls are de-duplicated via `filterDuplicateToolCalls()`.",
    "8. Contact memory and lead memory are updated via `applyToolCallsToContactMemory()` and `applyToolCallsToLeadMemory()`. The reply text is post-processed by `refineLocalReply()` which uses `deriveConversationPolicy()` to determine if the conversation is in 'qualify', 'capture_contact', 'resolve', or 'close' stage.",
    "9. The conversation is persisted to Prisma via `persistLocalConversation()` → creates/updates `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage` rows. The reply, contactMemory, and leadMemory are returned as JSON to the frontend.",
    "10. ChatWidget receives the response: `processToolCalls()` extracts tool calls and executes them via `runToolCall()` → dispatches custom DOM events (`workmat:open-section`, `workmat:contact-update`). The assistant reply is displayed with typing animation via `typeAssistantMessage()`.",
    "11. Step 4-10 repeats as the assistant progressively qualifies the lead: asks about work type, zone/postal code, urgency, location detail. Once enough context is gathered (`hasContactCaptureSignal`), it asks for name and phone.",
    "12. When the user signals farewell (says 'adiós', 'gracias') or clicks close → `closeChat('farewell')` is called in ChatWidget (line ~1439). This triggers `notifySessionClosed()` which `PATCH`es `/api/chat` with `action:'close'`.",
    "13. The PATCH handler (line ~2829) calls `findChatSessionForClose()` then `closeLocalChatSession()` / `closeOpenAiChatSession()` → `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~693). This updates the session status to 'completed'/'closed', then runs `syncChatSessionLeadState()` from `src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts`.",
    "14. `syncChatSessionLeadState()` (line ~453 in chatLeadTracking.ts) re-scans all messages for name/phone/email/workType/urgency/zone etc., builds a `LeadSummary`, and creates or updates a `Lead` record in Prisma. It also emits `LeadEvent` rows (chat_session_created, chat_session_closed).",
    "15. Back in `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~754 in route.ts): `extractLeadFromClosedChat()` builds final lead data. If SMTP is configured, `sendSmtpMail()` sends an HTML email with transcript to `notifyTo` (cesarmesaspain@gmail.com). For OpenAI provider, also appends the lead to Google Sheets via `appendClosedChatLeadToSheet()` using a JWT-authenticated Google Sheets API call."
  ],
  "state_transitions": [
    "CHAT_CLOSED → CHAT_OPEN (ChatWidget: chatOpen=false→true, greeting displayed)",
    "CHAT_OPEN → CHAT_AWAITING_REPLY (user submits message, loading=true/'Escribiendo...' shown)",
    "CHAT_AWAITING_REPLY → CHAT_QUALIFYING (assistant asks about work type, zone, urgency, etc.; leadMemory fields fill progressively)",
    "CHAT_QUALIFYING → CHAT_CAPTURING_CONTACT (once hasContactCaptureSignal=true, assistant asks for name+phone)",
    "CHAT_CAPTURING_CONTACT → CHAT_RESOLVING (both name and phone captured, assistant says specialist will contact)",
    "CHAT_RESOLVING → CHAT_CLOSING (user says farewell or inactivity timeout; farewell countdown shown)",
    "CHAT_CLOSING → CHAT_CLOSED (countdown expires or user cancels → closeChat('farewell') or closeChat('manual') called)",
    "CHAT_CLOSED → LEAD_PERSISTED (syncChatSessionLeadState() creates/updates Lead + LeadEvent in Prisma)",
    "LEAD_PERSISTED → LEAD_NOTIFIED (closeTrackedChatSession() sends email via SMTP, appends to Google Sheets)"
  ],
  "external_boundaries": [
    "OpenAI API (`api.openai.com/v1/responses`) — when provider is `'openai'`, used for AI response generation. See `openAiFetchJson()` in `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` at line ~126.",
    "Local AI endpoint (configurable `AI_BASE_URL` + `/chat` path) — when provider is `'local'`, used for local LLM inference. See `requestAiChatAttempt()` in route.ts at line ~1673.",
    "Vapi API (`/api/vapi_chat`) — when provider is `'vapi'`, proxies user requests to Vapi. See `forwardRequest()` in route.ts at line ~2660.",
    "SMTP / Resend — sending lead notification email to `notifyTo`. See `sendSmtpMail()` in `src/lib/mailing/provider.ts`, called from `closeTrackedChatSession()` at line ~754.",
    "Google Sheets API — appending lead rows to configured spreadsheet (OpenAI provider only). See `appendClosedChatLeadToSheet()` using `google.auth.JWT` at line ~641.",
    "Make.com API (`api.make.com`) — admin panel hooks/scenarios integration. See `src/app/api/admin/make/hooks/route.ts` at line ~42.",
    "Prisma (PostgreSQL) — persistence for ChatSession, ChatMessage, Lead, LeadEvent, Visitor entities. Used throughout the entire flow."
  ],
  "failure_points": [
    "Chat consent check fails → `ChatConsentModal` blocks message sending. User must accept consent (stored in localStorage `workmat-chat-consent`). File: ChatWidget.tsx line ~1222.",
    "LLM provider unavailable (local AI down, OpenAI quota exceeded 429, or Vapi timeout 504) → `getChatErrorMessage()` returns localized error string. Admin sees raw error. File: `chat/route.ts` `requestLocalChatReply()` line ~2294, `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` line ~2499.",
    "SMTP misconfigured or fails → email delivery skipped (`nextDeliveryState.emailError` set). No blocking, lead still persisted. File: `chat/route.ts` `closeTrackedChatSession()` line ~769.",
    "Google Sheets append fails → sheet append skipped (`nextDeliveryState.sheetError` set). Non-blocking. File: route.ts line ~797.",
    "Farewell detection false negative → user says goodbye but assistant doesn't detect it → manual close still works. File: ChatWidget.tsx `handleAssistantFarewellCandidate()` using `farewellTerms`.",
    "History overflow → `normalizeHistory()` keeps only last 8 messages; older context lost. File: route.ts line ~674.",
    "Conversation key collision → extremely unlikely with `crypto.randomUUID()`, but would merge chat sessions. File: ChatWidget.tsx line ~568."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "`src/app/page.tsx` — RSC entry point rendering `<HomeApp>`",
    "`src/app/components/HomeApp.tsx` — Shell that mounts `<ChatWidget>` on `chatBooted=true` (line ~439)",
    "`src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx` — Full frontend chat widget (~1600 lines). `handleSubmit()` POSTs to `/api/chat` (line ~1046). `closeChat()` PATCHes to `/api/chat` (line ~1439). `typeAssistantMessage()` renders replies (line ~1250). `processToolCalls()` executes assistant tools (line ~1335).",
    "`src/app/api/chat/route.ts` — Main API (~3400 lines). Contains `requestLocalChatReply()` (line ~2294), `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` (line ~2499), `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~693), `persistLocalConversation()` (line ~1566).",
    "`src/components/chat/chatPayload.ts` — Response parsing: `extractToolCalls()`, `sanitizeAssistantText()`, `extractErrorText()`",
    "`src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts` — `syncChatSessionLeadState()` (line ~453): re-scans messages, creates/updates Lead + LeadEvent in Prisma.",
    "`src/lib/chatLeadMemory.ts` — Browser-side lead memory via localStorage",
    "`src/lib/contactStorage.ts` — Browser-side contact memory via localStorage",
    "`src/lib/mailing/provider.ts` — `sendSmtpMail()` and `resolveSmtpRuntimeConfig()`",
    "`src/lib/companyProfile.ts` — Company contact data for system prompt injection",
    "`src/lib/chatSettings.ts` — Default system prompt and chat settings",
    "`src/lib/assistantBridge.ts` — Custom DOM events for section navigation from chat"
  ],
  "summary": "WORKMAT's core user-facing workflow is an AI-powered customer service chat that lives on the public website. A visitor opens the chat widget (mounted by HomeApp), converses with an AI assistant (running on local LLM, OpenAI, or Vapi), which progressively qualifies the lead by extracting work type, location, urgency, and contact information. The assistant's structured memory (contactMemory + leadMemory) drives the conversation policy: greet → qualify → capture contact → resolve → close. When the conversation ends (farewell/inactivity/manual), the backend syncs the lead to the database (Prisma Lead + LeadEvent), sends an HTML email notification via SMTP with full transcript, and for OpenAI also appends the lead to Google Sheets. External boundaries include OpenAI API, local AI endpoint, Vapi, SMTP/Resend, Google Sheets, and Make.com. Failure points (quota exceeded, SMTP misconfigured, timeout) are handled gracefully with fallback error messages and non-blocking delivery tracking."
}
```

flow: 1. User visits website → `src/app/page.tsx` renders `<HomeApp>` with hero layout. The hero CTA button ('Habla con WORKMAT') calls `handleOpenChat(null)` in `src/app/components/HomeApp.tsx` (line ~214).
2. `handleOpenChat` sets `chatBooted=true`, `chatOpen=true` → this mounts `<ChatWidget open={true} ... >` in `HomeApp.tsx` (line ~439).

3. ChatWidget (`src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx`) initializes: checks chat consent (`ChatConsentModal`), generates a `conversationKey` via `crypto.randomUUID()`, stores it in localStorage as `workmat-conversation-key`, and sends opening greeting (`queueAssistantMessages` with greeting lines).

4. User types a message and presses Enter → `handleSubmit()` in ChatWidget (line ~1046) validates input, calls `scheduleAssistantPresence(text)` to show 'Escribiendo...', then `POST`s JSON to `/api/chat` via `fetchWithTimeout`.

5. The API route `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` → `POST()` (line ~2680) reads runtime settings via `readChatRuntimeSettings()`. If provider is `'local'` → `requestLocalChatReply()` (line ~2294); if `'openai'` → `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` (line ~2499); if `'vapi'` → proxies to the Vapi endpoint.

6. In `requestLocalChatReply()`: the system prompt is built by `buildCompactChatSystemPrompt()` (line ~2185). The message history + new input is sent to the local LLM (Qwen etc.) via `requestAiChatAttempt()`. The LLM returns an answer containing `<json>...</json>` extraction and possibly `<tools>...</tools>` tool calls.

7. The answer is parsed by `parseLocalModelExtraction()` (extract structured lead/contact data) and `parseLocalToolCalls()` (extract function calls like `enviar_nombre_usuario`, `guardar_tipo_trabajo`, etc.). Tool calls are de-duplicated via `filterDuplicateToolCalls()`.

8. Contact memory and lead memory are updated via `applyToolCallsToContactMemory()` and `applyToolCallsToLeadMemory()`. The reply text is post-processed by `refineLocalReply()` which uses `deriveConversationPolicy()` to determine if the conversation is in 'qualify', 'capture_contact', 'resolve', or 'close' stage.

9. The conversation is persisted to Prisma via `persistLocalConversation()` → creates/updates `ChatSession` and `ChatMessage` rows. The reply, contactMemory, and leadMemory are returned as JSON to the frontend.

10. ChatWidget receives the response: `processToolCalls()` extracts tool calls and executes them via `runToolCall()` → dispatches custom DOM events (`workmat:open-section`, `workmat:contact-update`). The assistant reply is displayed with typing animation via `typeAssistantMessage()`.

11. Step 4-10 repeats as the assistant progressively qualifies the lead: asks about work type, zone/postal code, urgency, location detail. Once enough context is gathered (`hasContactCaptureSignal`), it asks for name and phone.

12. When the user signals farewell (says 'adiós', 'gracias') or clicks close → `closeChat('farewell')` is called in ChatWidget (line ~1439). This triggers `notifySessionClosed()` which `PATCH`es `/api/chat` with `action:'close'`.

13. The PATCH handler (line ~2829) calls `findChatSessionForClose()` then `closeLocalChatSession()` / `closeOpenAiChatSession()` → `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~693). This updates the session status to 'completed'/'closed', then runs `syncChatSessionLeadState()` from `src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts`.

14. `syncChatSessionLeadState()` (line ~453 in chatLeadTracking.ts) re-scans all messages for name/phone/email/workType/urgency/zone etc., builds a `LeadSummary`, and creates or updates a `Lead` record in Prisma. It also emits `LeadEvent` rows (chat_session_created, chat_session_closed).

15. Back in `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~754 in route.ts): `extractLeadFromClosedChat()` builds final lead data. If SMTP is configured, `sendSmtpMail()` sends an HTML email with transcript to `notifyTo` (cesarmesaspain@gmail.com). For OpenAI provider, also appends the lead to Google Sheets via `appendClosedChatLeadToSheet()` using a JWT-authenticated Google Sheets API call.
state_transitions: CHAT_CLOSED → CHAT_OPEN (ChatWidget: chatOpen=false→true, greeting displayed)
CHAT_OPEN → CHAT_AWAITING_REPLY (user submits message, loading=true/'Escribiendo...' shown)
CHAT_AWAITING_REPLY → CHAT_QUALIFYING (assistant asks about work type, zone, urgency, etc.; leadMemory fields fill progressively)
CHAT_QUALIFYING → CHAT_CAPTURING_CONTACT (once hasContactCaptureSignal=true, assistant asks for name+phone)
CHAT_CAPTURING_CONTACT → CHAT_RESOLVING (both name and phone captured, assistant says specialist will contact)
CHAT_RESOLVING → CHAT_CLOSING (user says farewell or inactivity timeout; farewell countdown shown)
CHAT_CLOSING → CHAT_CLOSED (countdown expires or user cancels → closeChat('farewell') or closeChat('manual') called)
CHAT_CLOSED → LEAD_PERSISTED (syncChatSessionLeadState() creates/updates Lead + LeadEvent in Prisma)
LEAD_PERSISTED → LEAD_NOTIFIED (closeTrackedChatSession() sends email via SMTP, appends to Google Sheets)
external_boundaries: OpenAI API (`api.openai.com/v1/responses`) — when provider is `'openai'`, used for AI response generation. See `openAiFetchJson()` in `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` at line ~126.
Local AI endpoint (configurable `AI_BASE_URL` + `/chat` path) — when provider is `'local'`, used for local LLM inference. See `requestAiChatAttempt()` in route.ts at line ~1673.
Vapi API (`/api/vapi_chat`) — when provider is `'vapi'`, proxies user requests to Vapi. See `forwardRequest()` in route.ts at line ~2660.
SMTP / Resend — sending lead notification email to `notifyTo`. See `sendSmtpMail()` in `src/lib/mailing/provider.ts`, called from `closeTrackedChatSession()` at line ~754.
Google Sheets API — appending lead rows to configured spreadsheet (OpenAI provider only). See `appendClosedChatLeadToSheet()` using `google.auth.JWT` at line ~641.
Make.com API (`api.make.com`) — admin panel hooks/scenarios integration. See `src/app/api/admin/make/hooks/route.ts` at line ~42.
Prisma (PostgreSQL) — persistence for ChatSession, ChatMessage, Lead, LeadEvent, Visitor entities. Used throughout the entire flow.
failure_points: Chat consent check fails → `ChatConsentModal` blocks message sending. User must accept consent (stored in localStorage `workmat-chat-consent`). File: ChatWidget.tsx line ~1222.

LLM provider unavailable (local AI down, OpenAI quota exceeded 429, or Vapi timeout 504) → `getChatErrorMessage()` returns localized error string. Admin sees raw error. File: `chat/route.ts` `requestLocalChatReply()` line ~2294, `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` line ~2499.
SMTP misconfigured or fails → email delivery skipped (`nextDeliveryState.emailError` set). No blocking, lead still persisted. File: `chat/route.ts` `closeTrackedChatSession()` line ~769.
Google Sheets append fails → sheet append skipped (`nextDeliveryState.sheetError` set). Non-blocking. File: route.ts line ~797.
Farewell detection false negative → user says goodbye but assistant doesn't detect it → manual close still works. File: ChatWidget.tsx `handleAssistantFarewellCandidate()` using `farewellTerms`.
History overflow → `normalizeHistory()` keeps only last 8 messages; older context lost. File: route.ts line ~674.
Conversation key collision → extremely unlikely with `crypto.randomUUID()`, but would merge chat sessions. File: ChatWidget.tsx line ~568.
evidence: `src/app/page.tsx` — RSC entry point rendering `<HomeApp>`
`src/app/components/HomeApp.tsx` — Shell that mounts `<ChatWidget>` on `chatBooted=true` (line ~439)

`src/components/chat/ChatWidget.tsx` — Full frontend chat widget (~1600 lines). `handleSubmit()` POSTs to `/api/chat` (line ~1046). `closeChat()` PATCHes to `/api/chat` (line ~1439). `typeAssistantMessage()` renders replies (line ~1250). `processToolCalls()` executes assistant tools (line ~1335).

`src/app/api/chat/route.ts` — Main API (~3400 lines). Contains `requestLocalChatReply()` (line ~2294), `requestOpenAiAssistantReply()` (line ~2499), `closeTrackedChatSession()` (line ~693), `persistLocalConversation()` (line ~1566).
`src/components/chat/chatPayload.ts` — Response parsing: `extractToolCalls()`, `sanitizeAssistantText()`, `extractErrorText()`
`src/lib/chatLeadTracking.ts` — `syncChatSessionLeadState()` (line ~453): re-scans messages, creates/updates Lead + LeadEvent in Prisma.
`src/lib/chatLeadMemory.ts` — Browser-side lead memory via localStorage
`src/lib/contactStorage.ts` — Browser-side contact memory via localStorage
`src/lib/mailing/provider.ts` — `sendSmtpMail()` and `resolveSmtpRuntimeConfig()`
`src/lib/companyProfile.ts` — Company contact data for system prompt injection
`src/lib/chatSettings.ts` — Default system prompt and chat settings
`src/lib/assistantBridge.ts` — Custom DOM events for section navigation from chat
result.summary: WORKMAT's core user-facing workflow is an AI-powered customer service chat that lives on the public website. A visitor opens the chat widget (mounted by HomeApp), converses with an AI assistant (running on local LLM, OpenAI, or Vapi), which progressively qualifies the lead by extracting work type, location, urgency, and contact information. The assistant's structured memory (contactMemory + leadMemory) drives the conversation policy: greet → qualify → capture contact → resolve → close. When the conversation ends (farewell/inactivity/manual), the backend syncs the lead to the database (Prisma Lead + LeadEvent), sends an HTML email notification via SMTP with full transcript, and for OpenAI also appends the lead to Google Sheets. External boundaries include OpenAI API, local AI endpoint, Vapi, SMTP/Resend, Google Sheets, and Make.com. Failure points (quota exceeded, SMTP misconfigured, timeout) are handled gracefully with fallback error messages and non-blocking delivery tracking.
